description.value:
Used as a replacement for skills rolled with base COOL, EMP, or WILL

Used for rolling in checks which involve force of will, charisma, general intelligence, or intution. Note that if the check is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers.

Used as a replacement for fighting or ranged weapon skills, excluding evasion. Note that this uses REF as a base for reasons involving initiative - set REF of the mook first, then consider that number when considering the Martial skill.
Used as a replacement for skills which rely on base Body or Dex, which are not covered by the Martial skill, and for Evasion.
Used for skills which rely on base INT or TECH.

An Exotic Very Heavy Pistol. Causes cyberware or electrical components to be rendered inoperable for a minute unless the target passes a DV15 Cybertech Check. Refer to the full item description on pg. 348.
A base interface for audio enhancements.
Built in agent. Audio only but can be linked with other cybergear.
Provides +2 to Human Perception and Interrogation checks. Can activate a special lie-detecting mode.
Provides immunity to deafness or sudden loud noises.
Provides +2 to Resist Torture/Drugs checks.
Provides immunity to inhalable gases or fumes.
Eyewear with two options slots for cybereye options.
Must be paired. Provides immunity to dazzling effects (like flashbangs).
Must be paired. +2 Perception and Lip Reading and Conceal/Reveal Object when using sight.
A cybernetical replacement arm. Comes with a free hand.
A cybernetical replacement arm. Comes with a free hand.
A concealable exotic net launcher. Can't hold anything with this hand while activated. (See Black Chrome pgs. 20-21 for more details)
A concealable exotic net launcher. Can't hold anything with this hand while activated. (See Black Chrome pgs. 20-21 for more details)
Netrunner deck. 9 slots.
Increases your BODY stat to 12 (adjusting your HP and Wound Threshold and Death Save)
Access to nervous system.
Speedware. +3 to Initiative for 1 min. 1hr cooldown.
Chipware. Removes Seriously Wounded penalties.
Plugs in the wrist or head to allow you to connect to machines/systems hands-free.
Net for the net launcher that grapples the target on hit. (See Black Chrome Pg. 20-21 for more details)

Intended as a direct substitute/improvement to regular armor, to compensate for ablation changes.

-4 to REF, DEX, and MOVE - these penalties do not stack with those for the helmet, and are reduced/improved by 1 for each BODY point the wearer has above 6 (change the armor penalty stat in the item settings tab).. Does not ablate while at or above 13 SP unless damage exceeds SP, the blow is from a melee weapon, or AP ammo is used.

Intended as a direct substitute/improvement to regular armor, to compensate for ablation changes.

-4 to REF, DEX, and MOVE - these penalties do not stack with those for the helmet, and are reduced/improved by 1 for each BODY point the wearer has above 6 (change the armor penalty stat in the item settings tab). Does not ablate while at or above 13 SP unless damage exceeds SP, the blow is from a melee weapon, or AP ammo is used.
A rechargeable (1 hour) battery pack, commonly used with Exotic weapons. Contains 8 shots and can be recharged.

information.notes:
Mook Tier - Tier 5x/MaxTac Elite - MaxTac arrive as a team, and work as a team - taken together, they're a lethal force to be reckoned with.

Assume that most of a MaxTac officer is EMP-hardened cyberware, even if that gear is not listed on the sheet. All of them are dependent on immunosuppressants and steady, daily or weekly doses of intensive therapy to keep them from going over the edge.

Ability - Synced Nervous Systems: Twice per initiative round, after one MaxTac officer takes an action, another officer may immediately move or take an action.

Ability - Runner Reflexes: Bullets can be dodged using the Physical skill. Additionally, the cyberdeck can be used to disable foes. After jacking into an enemy as an action (note: this is extremely painful and invasive for the person in question, as it is not an intended function for most cyberware and actually resembles a directional, high-energy laser blast), the runner can disable one piece of cyberware on the target each turn. These pieces of cyberware are reenabled, if the runner jacks out.

Gang-Wide Ability - Threat Rating: When the NCPD is first alerted to a disturbance in a district, a threat rating is immediately assigned. When the reinforcements at that tier of threat are defeated, forced to retreat, or assess the situation, the threat rating increases by 1 or more, and the reinforcements associated with that rating are immediately dispatched, arriving in 1d3 minutes.

Threat rating is not assigned in combat zones, the reclaimed perimeter or the NorCal Military Base, and cannot exceed 2 in the overpacked suburbs.

  • Tier 1: No reinforcements are sent. Created when an NCPD officer is engaged or feels threatened.

  • Tier 2 - Armed Response: two NCPD Patrol Cars are dispatched, each containing two Patrol Officers.

  • Tier 3 - Perimeter Control: three NCPD Patrol Cars are dispatched, each containing three Patrol Officers and a SWAT Officer. These officers will create a perimeter and assess the situation while attempting to prevent anyone from leaving the scene, but will not directly engage agitators unless they attempt to breach the perimeter, instead calling in a Suppression team to take down dangerous players while notifying MaxTac to remain on standby.

  • Tier 4 - Suppression Dispatch: an NCPD Armored Transport is dispatched, containing an eight-person team of SWAT Officers.

  • Tier 5 - MaxTac Response: An A/V-4 is dispatched, containing a four person team of MaxTac Officers. They do not negotiate, do not discriminate, do not take bribes, and do not take prisoners if the perps don’t surrender peacefully. They are the law.

You think MaxTac will keep you safe? Hah! You’ve obviously never seen them in action. Collateral damage is their middle name - all of it blamed on the psycho they capture or kill, of course. Yes, I said capture - where do you think the chromed out monsters that fill their ranks come from, eh? Recruit from the corrupt NCPD or off the streets? No, choom, there’s two ways to get into MaxTac - prove yourself as an edgerunner and be ready to be chromed out of your mind, or prove yourself as a psycho and let yourself be taken alive. Assuming they have an opening, the day they come to put you down, eh?
